design_philosophy.core_loop: Arena-based deathmatch: spawn, acquire weapons/power-ups from map pickups, engage in fast-paced combat, respawn and repeat. Strafe-jumping and rocket-jumping enable advanced movement mechanics that reward player skill.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time multiplayer combat with weapon pickup scarcity and map control. Single-player mode simulates this via progressive difficulty tiers of bot opponents.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become the greatest warrior in the Arena Eternal, competing against legendary fighters in skill-based one-on-one duels and free-for-all deathmatches.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately stripped of story campaign in favor of pure multiplayer focus. Minimalist design philosophy prioritizes competitive gameplay over narrative. Every system (weapon balance, map symmetry, bot progression) serves esports viability. No concessions to single-player storytelling.

difficulty.lives_system: Respawn-based; no lives limit. Death results in weapon loss and spawn with default loadout (gauntlet, machine gun).
difficulty.checkpointing: Single-player tournament mode progresses through fixed tier structure (Tier 0 to Tier 7), with bots increasing in difficulty. Each tier represents a checkpoint-like progression gate.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Handicap system allows multiplayer players to level competition. Single-player tier progression is linear with no difficulty selection shown in source, but bot skill scales explicitly from Crash (easiest) to Xaero (hardest).

progression.upgrade_system: No persistent upgrades. Progression through weapon acquisition via map pickups. Quad Damage power-up provides temporary combat advantage. All players spawn equal.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Single-player unlocks successive bot tiers and more complex arenas as player advances through tournament brackets. Estimated 8 tiers total.
replayability_hooks: Multiplayer community remains active; multiple game modes (FFA, TDM, Tournament, CTF)
Customizable settings (field of view, texture detail, enemy models) allow personalized experiences
Symmetrical CTF maps enable competitive replay
Bot difficulty tiers can be replayed for skill mastery
Professional esports scene provides competitive benchmark

world_building_techniques: Brief opening story: warriors abducted by the Vadrigar race to fight in Arena Eternal
Bot character models and biographical information maintain continuity with Doom/Quake series legacy
Gothic and technological map architecture (familiar Quake aesthetic)
Iconic weapons (Quad Damage, BFG, rocket launcher) carry series lore
Introduction video shows abduction of character Sarge during last stand

boss_design.philosophy: No traditional boss battles in single-player. Tournament maps function as 'boss encounters'—1v1 duels against progressively skilled bot opponents. Xaero (Tier 7) serves as final opponent.
notable_bosses: Xaero (Tier 7) - final tier opponent, described as toughest

faction_indicators: Team Deathmatch: two-team structure (typically 4v4) with explicit team scoring
Capture the Flag: symmetrical base design signals opposing factions fighting for territorial control
Tournament/1v1: individual champions rise through competitive hierarchy
danger_signals: Weapon spawning at fixed map locations creates power-up hotspots where violence concentrates
Dropped weapons after death signal combat intensity and strategic loot contests
Arena Eternal framing: constant warfare as entertainment implies normalized violence
BFG super-weapon in hard-to-reach locations signals dangerous territory
environmental_storytelling: Gothic and technological architecture visualizes merge of Doom and Quake universes
Map complexity increases per tier, signaling progression into more dangerous realms
Weapon placement distribution suggests strategic arena design (e.g., railgun for long-range sightlines, lightning gun for close-quarters corridors)
Symmetrical CTF maps reinforce balanced, organized conflict structure
